live_price_hotel_fallback.before_phase_5: None (confirmed by code inspection of the unmodified function)
live_price_hotel_fallback.interpretation: Ladder now falls through to price_estimator's hotel rung instead of returning nothing.

transport_compare_cab_regression.regression_math: 300 + 16 * 286.54 = 4884.64 -> rounds to 4885, exactly what the old hardcoded _CAB_BASE_FARE/_CAB_RATE_PER_KM constants would have produced.
transport_compare_cab_regression.interpretation: price_estimator.estimate('cab', ...) is a true drop-in replacement for the removed inline formula, unambiguous city pair used deliberately to avoid the separate _resolve_city substring-collision bug (see below).

conversation_engine_nameerror_fix.bug: conversation_engine.py::_compute_recommended_budget_inr referenced DEST_TIER_RATES and PURPOSE_BUDGET_MULTIPLIERS, neither of which was ever imported into that file — a live NameError waiting to fire the first time _build_optional_prefilled ran on a draft with no budget_inr in metadata.
conversation_engine_nameerror_fix.fix: Broken duplicate deleted; conversation_engine.py now imports and calls the canonical recommendations.recommended_budget_inr(draft, purpose).
result: typical domestic costs for this destination
conversation_engine_nameerror_fix.interpretation: Clean tuple, no exception — grep confirms conversation_engine.py no longer references DEST_TIER_RATES/PURPOSE_BUDGET_MULTIPLIERS/_compute_recommended_budget_inr at all; it calls recommended_budget_inr directly (conversation_engine.py:656).

separate_pre_existing_bug_confirmed_stil.description: _resolve_city('Agra') in transport_compare.py (unedited by Phase 5) resolves via plain icontains substring match with no exact-match-first guard, so 'Agra' matches 'Lagrange, US' (Agra is a literal substring of L-AGRA-NGE) instead of Agra, India.
separate_pre_existing_bug_confirmed_stil.status_as_of_this_evidence_pass: FIXED — apps/planner/services/transport_compare.py::_resolve_city now tries name__iexact first, falling back to name__icontains only when no exact match exists. Confirmed by direct code read in the active worktree (backend/apps/planner/services/transport_compare.py). This was the subject of a separate, already-completed session (see memory 'Planner city icontains substring-collision bug').
separate_pre_existing_bug_confirmed_stil.caveat: The Phase 5 code itself (in this main checkout's uncommitted tree) introduces a NEW instance of the identical bug pattern in apps/reference/services/live_price.py::_resolve_observation_fk's cab branch (line ~137: City.objects.filter(name__icontains=origin).first(), no iexact-first guard). Flagged separately below — not fixed as part of generating this evidence, per the repo's 'record, don't silently fix out-of-scope findings' norm.
